title: Arrays and multidimensional arrays deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Arrays and multidimensional arrays; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Arrays and multidimensional arrays: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A two-dimensional Java array is one rectangular block, and every row always has the same length as the outer array.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.
A two-dimensional Java array is one rectangular block, and every row always has the same length as the outer array.

answer.explanation: Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.

items.title: Arrays and multidimensional arrays: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Java: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'array of row references'. Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
items.template: Treat a multidimensional array as an [[checkpoint]], allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.

answer.explanation: Treat a multidimensional array as an array of row references, allocate and bound-check each row independently, and never infer column count from the outer length.

items.title: Arrays and multidimensional arrays: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read row count, then for each row read its length and integer elements. Print each ragged row sum separated by one space.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Each row is allocated and bounded independently, so empty and differently sized rows remain valid.
